commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=44af7b7054c77c52d5ae40299ea2fd2204a98e48)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an opt-in MiMo local JSONL usage tracker/cache, wires a `mimo.local` auto fallback into provider and CLI source selection, suppresses web billing notes for local source, documents setup, edits the changelog, and adds focused MiMo/CLI/script tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. for the central feature request. Source inspection shows current main is web-only for MiMo and PR head adds the local fallback; I did not run runtime validation because this is a read-only review and live provider probes are discouraged by repo policy.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human handling remains because merge depends on maintainer acceptance of the external local-accounting workflow and latest-head real behavior proof, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: feat(mimo): opt-in local tracker fallback for MiMo card
target.body:
## Summary
- Add an implicit opt-in local fallback so the MiMo card surfaces meaningful data (lifetime/weekly token sums + progress bar) when the platform.xiaomimimo.com SSO cookie path is unavailable, instead of stuck on `Xiaomi MiMo login required`.
- `MiMoWebFetchStrategy.fetch()` wraps existing cookie logic in try/catch and falls back to `MiMoLocalUsageFallback.snapshot()` only when `~/.codexbar/mimo-local-usage.json` exists (zero behavior change for users who do not opt in).
- Suppress the "Balance updates in near-real time / Daily billing finalizes at 07:00 UTC" footer when `sourceLabel == "local"` since neither applies to local accounting.
- Ship `Scripts/mimo-usage.py` (Python 3, no external deps) as a sample tracker â€” scans `~/.claude-envs/mimo/.claude/projects/**/*.jsonl` for any Claude Code wrapper pointed at MiMo (e.g. `cc-mimo` with `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=https://token-plan-sgp.xiaomimimo.com/anthropic`) and aggregates `input_tokens` / `output_tokens` / `cache_read_input_tokens` per today / week / all_time window.
- Documented under `docs/mimo.md` â†’ "Local fallback (opt-in)".

## Why
The MiMo provider currently only reaches platform.xiaomimimo.com via SSO cookies, which break in common macOS scenarios:

- Chrome session cookies (`api-platform_serviceToken` etc.) clear on Chrome relaunch (they're session-level: `expires=0` in Chrome SQLite).
- `Disable Keychain access` / TCC blocks reading Chrome Safe Storage to decrypt the cookie value.
- The user drives MiMo inference only through a wrapper and never logs into platform from a browser.

When this happens the MiMo card shows `Xiaomi MiMo login required` indefinitely with no escape hatch â€” `tp-`-prefixed inference API keys (`token-plan-sgp.xiaomimimo.com/anthropic`) cannot query platform quota; they are two independent auth systems per Xiaomi's own docs.

The local fallback is **implicit opt-in**: it activates only when the cache file exists. Users without a local wrapper / tracker see no change; current behavior preserved exactly.

## Tests
- Added `MiMoLocalUsageFallbackTests` covering:
  - cache file missing â†’ `snapshot()` returns nil â†’ fallback skipped
  - cache file malformed JSON â†’ returns nil â†’ fallback skipped
  - normal parse with progress bar (today/week/lifetime/sessions in planCode + tokenUsed/tokenLimit/tokenPercent for the progress bar)
  - idle-week edge case (week=0, allTime>0 â†’ progress bar empty with lifetime as baseline)

## Validation
- `swift build --target CodexBarCore` passes locally.
- `swift build --target CodexBar` passes locally.
- Local `swift test --filter MiMoLocalUsageFallbackTests` is blocked before reaching the new tests by the local Command Line Tools environment (`KeyboardShortcuts` `#Preview` macro plugins are unavailable without full Xcode); CI should be the test signal.

## Scope
No changes to OAuth/cookie paths, provider selection ordering, settings UI, `Package.swift`, or non-MiMo providers. Behavior is identical to current `main` when `~/.codexbar/mimo-local-usage.json` does not exist.
target.author: leolin990405

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
